regulations.name: EU AI Act (2024)
regulations.relevance: Classifies AI systems that manipulate behaviour as unacceptable risk (Article 5). Subliminal techniques that materially distort behaviour are prohibited.

regulations.name: EU Digital Services Act
regulations.relevance: Requires very large platforms to assess systemic risks including manipulation. Mandates transparency in algorithmic recommendation.

regulations.name: GDPR (Articles 5, 6, 22)
regulations.relevance: Requires lawful basis for data processing. Article 22 protects against solely automated decision-making. Relevant when persuasive content is algorithmically targeted based on personal data profiling.

regulations.name: EU Unfair Commercial Practices Directive (2005/29/EC)
regulations.relevance: Prohibits misleading and aggressive commercial practices. Fabricated scarcity, false authority claims, and artificial urgency may constitute unfair practices.

regulations.name: US FTC Act Section 5
regulations.relevance: Prohibits unfair or deceptive acts. Dark patterns enforcement is active and expanding.

regulations.name: US FTC Endorsement Guides (16 CFR Part 255)
regulations.relevance: Requires truthful endorsements and disclosures. Fake expert claims, fabricated testimonials, and undisclosed material connections violate these guidelines.

regulations.name: California Consumer Privacy Act (CCPA/CPRA)
regulations.relevance: Grants consumers rights over personal information used for profiling and targeted advertising. Relevant when persuasion tactics are algorithmically personalised.

regulations.name: US COPPA (Children's Online Privacy Protection Act)
regulations.relevance: Protects children under 13 from unfair data practices. Manipulative tactics targeting minors face heightened scrutiny.

regulations.name: Canada Competition Act (Sections 52, 74)
regulations.relevance: Prohibits false or misleading representations and deceptive marketing practices. Section 52 (criminal) and Section 74 (civil) cover false claims, misleading price representations, and deceptive performance claims.

regulations.name: Canada PIPEDA / CPPA
regulations.relevance: Governs collection, use, and disclosure of personal information in commercial activities. The proposed Consumer Privacy Protection Act (CPPA) strengthens enforcement with significant penalties. Relevant when persuasion is driven by personal data profiling.

regulations.name: Canada CASL (Anti-Spam Legislation)
regulations.relevance: Prohibits sending commercial electronic messages without consent and installing software without consent. Requires clear identification and unsubscribe mechanisms. Deceptive subject lines and false sender information are prohibited.

regulations.name: Canadian Provincial Consumer Protection Acts
regulations.relevance: Provincial consumer protection legislation (e.g., Ontario CPA, Quebec CPA, BC BPCPA) prohibits unfair practices, false representations, and unconscionable conduct in consumer transactions.

regulations.name: Australian Online Safety Act 2021
regulations.relevance: Covers deceptive design patterns and harmful online content. The eSafety Commissioner has powers to investigate and enforce removal of harmful material.

regulations.name: Australian Consumer Law (Schedule 2, CCA 2010)
regulations.relevance: Prohibits misleading or deceptive conduct (Section 18), unconscionable conduct (Sections 20-22), and false representations (Sections 29-37). Covers unfair contract terms and product safety.

regulations.name: Australian Privacy Act 1988 (APPs)
regulations.relevance: Australian Privacy Principles govern collection, use, disclosure, and security of personal information. Relevant when persuasion is driven by personal data profiling or behavioural targeting.

regulations.name: Australian Consumer Data Right (CDR)
regulations.relevance: Data transparency obligations for how consumer behavioural data is collected, used, and shared. Relevant in banking, energy, and telecommunications contexts.
